name: THORACIC OUTLET SYNDROME
definition: neurovascular syndrome caused by compression of subclavian vessels + brachial plexus EFFORT THROMBOSIS primary thrombosis of subclavian vein at costoclavicular junction (= subtype of thoracic
discriminating_features: complete cessation of flow in one position
complete disappearance of pulse in one position
effacement of fat planes around compressed plexus
abnormal intramuscular course of brachial plexus components

general: complete cessation of flow in one position
complete disappearance of pulse in one position
ANGIO: abnormal course of distal subclavian artery
poststenotic dilatation of distal subclavian artery
MR: effacement of fat planes around compressed plexus
abnormal intramuscular course of brachial plexus components
